spirit-guardians.canonicalEvidence: Speed is halved in the Emanation, and whenever the Emanation enters a creature space and whenever a creature enters the Emanation or ends its turn there, the creature must make a Wisdom saving throw. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
spirit-guardians.structuredState: Save/damage are represented; no speed-halving or once-per-turn area trigger fields.
spirit-guardians.jsonState: Runtime has immediate Wisdom save half damage but no speed halving or once-per-turn area triggers.
spirit-guardians.issue: Spirit Guardians damage is partially represented, but movement slowing and recurring once-per-turn triggers are not.
spirit-guardians.recommendedTemplateChange: Add emanation speed modifier and once-per-turn area trigger fields.
spirit-guardians.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime speedMultiplier 0.5 in emanation and area triggers for emanation enters, creature enters, or creature ends turn, once per turn.

spirit-guardians.canonicalEvidence: Protective spirits flit around you in a 15-foot Emanation for the duration.
spirit-guardians.structuredState: No summoned spirit/persistent emanation entity fields.
spirit-guardians.issue: The protective spirits are a persistent emanation presentation; the mechanical gaps are tracked as area trigger, damage type, and target exclusion rows.
spirit-guardians.recommendedTemplateChange: No separate summon field required unless visual/entity presentation becomes runtime-significant.
spirit-guardians.recommendedJsonChange: No separate summon runtime change required for this row.

spirit-guardians.canonicalEvidence: When you cast this spell, you can designate creatures to be unaffected by it. Any other creature...
spirit-guardians.jsonState: Runtime target filter has no exclusion list chosen at cast time.
spirit-guardians.issue: Spirit Guardians lets the caster designate unaffected creatures at cast time.
spirit-guardians.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime excludedTargets chosenOnCast for unaffected creatures.

spirit-guardians.canonicalEvidence: Their spectral form appears angelic or fey, or fiendish if evil.
spirit-guardians.jsonState: Runtime does not type descriptive spectral form.
spirit-guardians.issue: The exact spectral appearance is deferred as presentation; alignment-linked damage/form choice remains actionable in choice rows.
spirit-guardians.recommendedTemplateChange: No presentation-only field required for this goal.

stinking-cloud.canonicalEvidence: Each creature that starts its turn in the Sphere must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the Poisoned condition until the end of the current turn. While Poisoned in this way, the creature cannot take an action or a Bonus Action.
stinking-cloud.structuredState: Save Stat: Constitution; Conditions Applied: Poisoned; no action/bonus action lockout field.
stinking-cloud.jsonState: Runtime now uses turn_start Constitution save to Poisoned without fake escape/repeat, but no action/bonus action lockout or end-current-turn duration.
stinking-cloud.issue: The save and Poisoned condition are partially represented; action/bonus action lockout and exact duration remain unmodeled.
stinking-cloud.recommendedTemplateChange: Add short condition duration and action/bonus action restriction fields.
stinking-cloud.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime poisoned rider cannotTakeActionOrBonusAction and duration until end of current turn.

stinking-cloud.jsonState: Runtime JSON has no visibility effect.
stinking-cloud.issue: This detector row is not healing; the real issue is heavily obscured area visibility.
stinking-cloud.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime environmentalChange heavilyObscured in area.

stinking-cloud.canonicalEvidence: The cloud lingers in the air for the duration or until a strong wind disperses it.
stinking-cloud.jsonState: Runtime JSON has no wind dispersal condition.
stinking-cloud.issue: Stinking Cloud can be dispersed by strong wind, but that environmental ending condition is not represented.
stinking-cloud.recommendedTemplateChange: Add environmental dispersal fields for gas/cloud effects.

stinking-cloud.canonicalEvidence: The cloud lingers in the air for the duration or until a strong wind disperses it.
stinking-cloud.structuredState: Duration is represented; wind dispersal is tracked separately.
stinking-cloud.jsonState: Runtime duration is represented; wind dispersal is not.
stinking-cloud.issue: The lingering cloud duration is ordinary duration data; this row is closed in favor of the environmental wind-dispersal row.
stinking-cloud.recommendedTemplateChange: No aftermath field required for this row.

summon-lesser-demons.canonicalEvidence: Roll on the table to determine what appears. The DM chooses the demons, and you choose the unoccupied spaces you can see within range where they appear.
summon-lesser-demons.structuredState: Effect Type: SUMMON; no random table, DM choice, or placement choice fields.
summon-lesser-demons.jsonState: Runtime JSON has one prose utility effect.
summon-lesser-demons.issue: Summon Lesser Demons uses a random summon-count/CR table, DM monster selection, and caster placement choices that are not typed.
summon-lesser-demons.recommendedTemplateChange: Add random summon table, DM choice, and chosen placement fields.
summon-lesser-demons.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime summonTable d6 outcomes, dmChoosesCreature, and casterChosenUnoccupiedSpaces.

summon-lesser-demons.canonicalEvidence: You choose the unoccupied spaces you can see within range where they appear.
summon-lesser-demons.structuredState: Valid Targets: location; no unoccupied/visible multi-space placement fields.
summon-lesser-demons.jsonState: Runtime targeting ground with maxTargets 1 cannot represent multiple unoccupied visible spaces.
summon-lesser-demons.issue: The placement targets are multiple unoccupied visible spaces, but runtime has one ground target.
summon-lesser-demons.recommendedTemplateChange: Add multi-placement target fields for summons.
summon-lesser-demons.recommendedJsonChange: Add runtime placementTargets unoccupied visible spaces within 60 feet with count from summon table.
